target.title: feat: add tools.fs.roots — per-agent filesystem roots with access modes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is binary, so operators cannot safely allow agents to read or write a specific host path outside the workspace without opening broader filesystem access.
- Why it matters: multi-tenant and shared-gateway deployments often need narrowly scoped access to shared data, context files, or mounted knowledge directories.
- What changed: added `tools.fs.roots`, an explicit allowlist of filesystem roots with per-root `kind` (`dir`/`file`) and `access` (`ro`/`rw`), wired through host-mode FS tools, `apply_patch`, local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery paths, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Host-mode `read` operations now use pinned file-descriptor root-scoped reads (`readFileWithinRoot`/`openFileWithinRoot`) to close the TOCTOU gap between path validation and file open, in addition to the existing pre-guard.
- Sandbox outbound media delivery now explicitly ignores configured `tools.fs.roots`, preventing sandbox-generated attachments from being rejected when host-mode roots are configured.
- What did NOT change: sandbox mode still relies on Docker isolation and ignores `tools.fs.roots`; `exec` sandboxing is unchanged; `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` behavior is unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: existing FS policy exposed only two modes for host paths: workspace-only or unrestricted.
- Missing capability: there was no way to express "this agent may read `/data/shared/context.md` but may not read the rest of the host".
- Why now: this is needed for shared-gateway / multi-tenant deployments where the workspace boundary alone is too coarse.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: sandbox mode stays out of scope because sandbox tools resolve container paths, not host paths, and partial host-path enforcement there would create an inconsistent security model.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files:
  - `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `src/media/load-options.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-pinned-write-helper.spawn-error.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.unlink-helper-error.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - lexical dir/file root matching and ro/rw enforcement
  - canonical-root precedence over alias roots for host-mode mutation paths
  - unlink/delete behavior preserving symlink-entry semantics while still respecting stricter canonical roots
  - roots precedence over `workspaceOnly` for host-mode FS access
  - sandbox-mode fallback still honoring `workspaceOnly`
  - rooted local image/PDF/reference-image loads outside the workspace
  - rooted outbound local-media and reply-delivery paths preserving file-vs-dir root semantics
  - rooted prompt-image auto-load outside the workspace
  - host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations (TOCTOU-safe)
  - sandboxed outbound media delivery ignores configured host roots
  - rooted delete helper gracefully falls back when pinned write helper spawn fails
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the risky surfaces here are policy resolution, host-path mutation, and media path propagation, so targeted seam tests catch the actual failure modes without requiring a full live deployment.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config surface: `tools.fs.roots` on global `tools.fs` and per-agent `tools.fs`.
- Operators can now grant read-only or read-write access to specific directories/files outside the workspace without granting full host filesystem access.
- Host-mode `read` / `write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` now respect those explicit roots.
- Local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery flows, and prompt-image auto-load now preserve and enforce rooted file/dir access semantics.
- When both `roots` and `workspaceOnly` are set, `roots` takes precedence for host-mode FS access and a warning is logged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds a new operator-controlled path restriction model for host-mode FS access. Risk is policy misunderstanding or incomplete enforcement across FS-adjacent surfaces. Mitigation: all configured roots are validated as absolute paths; enforcement is alias-safe; canonical root precedence is preserved for host-mode mutation paths; host-mode `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load are covered; host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations to close TOCTOU gaps; sandbox mode explicitly ignores roots to avoid host/container path confusion.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15 / darwin 25.3.0
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for core FS policy; targeted local media tests use mocked image/PDF/image-generation providers
- Integration/channel (if any): host-mode FS tools + local media loading + outbound local-media reply flows
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  "tools": {
    "fs": {
      "roots": [
        { "path": "/opt/app/workspaces/agent-1", "kind": "dir", "access": "rw" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/invoices", "kind": "dir", "access": "ro" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/context.md", "kind": "file", "access": "ro" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with `tools.fs.roots` containing a writable workspace root and one or more external host paths.
2. Attempt `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` and local media access both inside and outside those roots.
3. Attempt outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows with both dir roots and exact file roots.
4. Attempt prompt-image auto-load from a rooted external image path.
5. Attempt the same config in sandbox mode.

### Expected

- Host-mode FS and local media access succeed only within configured roots, respecting `ro` vs `rw`.
- Outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows preserve file-vs-dir root semantics and explicit deny-all behavior.
- Prompt-image auto-load accepts explicitly rooted host paths.
- Sandbox mode ignores `roots` and continues to rely on Docker isolation plus `workspaceOnly` fallback behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no granular host-path policy between workspace-only and unrestricted host access.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`):
  - merged `origin/main` at `631552c554`
  - `pnpm config:docs:gen`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
  - `pnpm check`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.memory-flush-forwarding.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "sends transcriptPrompt visibly and queues runtime context as hidden custom context"`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts -t "ignores host roots for sandboxed read/write/edit tools"`
- Additional focused regressions were exercised during review for:
  - fs-root resolution / precedence
  - alias-safe host mutations and unlink/delete behavior
  - media root propagation and explicit deny-all handling
  - prompt-image and local-media rooted access
  - outbound local-media / reply-delivery adapters
  - pinned-FD host reads (TOCTOU closure)
  - sandbox outbound media ignoring configured roots
- What you did **not** verify on `7b771d448d`:
  - full local `pnpm test`
  - `pnpm build`
  - live end-to-end multi-tenant deployment behavior beyond local seam tests
  - `pnpm check:changed` did not complete locally: it passed type/lint/guard phases and 2,450 tests before a local Vitest gateway worker hit `ERR_WORKER_OUT_OF_MEMORY`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `tools.fs.roots` from config or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/pi-tools.ts`, `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.ts`, `src/agents/apply-patch.ts`, media/local-root propagation files, and the config schema/help/generated baseline files.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: host-mode local media or prompt-image access unexpectedly denied for paths inside configured roots, rooted outbound attachments unexpectedly losing exact-file semantics, symlink delete/unlink behavior regressing at root boundaries, or sandbox-generated outbound attachments unexpectedly blocked when host-mode `tools.fs.roots` is configured.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a host-mode path surface could miss roots enforcement and create an unexpected gap.
  - Mitigation: added targeted regression tests for host tools, `apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Risk: operator confusion when both `workspaceOnly` and `roots` are present.
  - Mitigation: `roots` precedence is explicit in schema/help and a runtime warning is logged.
- Risk: `exec` still bypasses FS roots through shell commands.
  - Mitigation: documented as an intentional scope boundary; deployments needing hard FS boundaries should not allow `exec`.

## AI assistance

- [x] AI-assisted
- Testing: locally revalidated on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`) with `pnpm check`, focused media/WhatsApp review-thread regressions, and the two prior PR-specific filtered tests; full `pnpm test` and `pnpm build` were not rerun on this final merged head; GitHub CI is green on `7b771d448d` (74 successful, 9 skipped) after the latest review fix.




## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `tools.fs.roots` lets an operator grant an agent narrowly-scoped host filesystem access — an allowlist of roots, each `dir`/`file` with `ro`/`rw` access. Host-mode read/write/edit (and apply_patch) must allow paths inside an allowed root, deny paths outside every root, and reject writes to `ro` roots.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `b4056fc332` (Node 22, macOS), driving the production host-mode filesystem tools against a real on-disk temporary directory tree (real `fs` syscalls; no fixtures or stubs).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a real temp tree (an `rw` root `shared-rw`, an `ro` root `shared-ro`, and a `secret/` directory outside every root with a real file in it), built the production tool set via `createOpenClawCodingTools({ config: { tools: { fs: { roots: [...] } } } })`, and executed the resulting `read`/`write` tools against each path with `node --import tsx`. Live console output below.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
config tools.fs.roots:
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-rw, kind: dir, access: rw }
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro, kind: dir, access: ro }
workspace (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/workspace
forbidden dir (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret

=== host-mode reads ===
READ  inside rw root  (shared-rw/notes.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "rw-root content"
READ  inside ro root  (shared-ro/policy.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "ro-root content"
READ  OUTSIDE roots   (secret/passwords.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/passwords.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots

=== host-mode writes ===
WRITE inside rw root  (shared-rw/created.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, on disk: true
WRITE inside ro root  (shared-ro/hack.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro/hack.txt' is inside read-only root '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro'  (on disk: false)
WRITE OUTSIDE roots   (secret/exfil.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/exfil.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots  (on disk: false)

verify: secret/passwords.txt still says: "TOP SECRET — outside every allowed root"
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Reads inside the `rw` and `ro` roots returned the real file contents. A read of `secret/passwords.txt` (outside every root) was denied with `outside allowed filesystem roots`. A write inside the `rw` root landed on disk (`on disk: true`); a write to the `ro` root was denied (`inside read-only root`) and a write outside every root was denied — both left nothing on disk (`on disk: false`), and the out-of-root secret file was byte-for-byte unchanged afterward.
- **What was not tested:** Sandbox mode is out of scope by design — sandbox ignores `tools.fs.roots` and relies on Docker isolation — so it was not exercised here. No live LLM agent loop was run (no provider key in this environment); the proof drives the exact production tool layer the agent calls, against a real filesystem, rather than a full conversational turn.

target.title: [AI] fix(daemon): set default NODE_OPTIONS --max-old-space-size in gateway service env
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw daemon install` now generates `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192` for managed gateway and node services, preventing the OOM crash-loop caused by Node's default ~4 GB heap under sustained load. Ambient host `NODE_OPTIONS` (including `--require`, `--inspect`, `--expose-gc`) are intentionally dropped from the service environment.

- Problem: Gateway crash-loops with default Node heap (~4 GB) under sustained 30-agent load. `openclaw daemon install` did not set any heap configuration.
- Solution: Generate a fixed `--max-old-space-size=8192` in managed service `NODE_OPTIONS`. Drop ambient host `NODE_OPTIONS` to prevent startup-control flags from persisting into services.
- What changed: `src/daemon/service-env.ts` (+14: `resolveServiceNodeOptions` helper, hard-coded 8192 MB default), `src/daemon/service-env.test.ts` (+67: heap default and ambient NODE_OPTIONS drop coverage)
- What did NOT change: No config key, env var, or CLI flag for heap tuning. Operators who need a different heap size can use the existing `OPENCLAW_WRAPPER` escape hatch or edit the generated service file directly. A durable heap-size surface is deferred to a follow-up.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #96203
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Under sustained load with 30+ concurrent agents, the default Node.js heap (~4 GB on 64-bit systems) causes the gateway to enter an OOM crash-loop. Users must manually edit systemd service files to add `--max-old-space-size`. This PR makes `openclaw daemon install` set a safe 8192 MB default automatically, matching the operational guidance already documented in the issue.

The `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` env var override was removed per ClawSweeper review — an env-var-only override is not durable across `daemon install --force` or updates. A persistent heap-size surface (config key or CLI flag) is deferred to a follow-up. For now, operators needing a different value can use `OPENCLAW_WRAPPER` or edit the generated service file.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Gateway daemon service now receives `--max-old-space-size=8192` in managed `NODE_OPTIONS`; ambient host `NODE_OPTIONS` flags are dropped
- Real environment tested: Linux 4.19, Node 22.19, branch `fix/gateway-daemon-heap-96203` @ `8774007`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/service-env.test.ts --run
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/service-env.test.ts --run
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.daemon.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  94 passed (94)
   Start at  19:16:53
   Duration  1.02s
```

- Observed result after fix:

| Test scenario | Result |
|---|---|
| Default gateway install (no env) | `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192` |
| Ambient `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=16384` | `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192` (ambient dropped) |
| Ambient `NODE_OPTIONS=--require /tmp/evil.js` | `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192` (no `--require`) |
| Ambient `NODE_OPTIONS=--inspect=0.0.0.0:9229` | `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192` (no `--inspect`) |
| `buildNodeServiceEnvironment` | `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192` |

- What was not tested: Full 30-agent OOM crash-loop reproduction; live `daemon install` systemd unit generation with `systemctl`

## Root Cause

Node.js default heap size (~4 GB on 64-bit) is insufficient for the gateway under sustained multi-agent load. Prior to this PR, `openclaw daemon install` generated no `NODE_OPTIONS` at all, leaving the service at Node's default.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

After `openclaw daemon install` (or reinstall), the managed service now runs with `--max-old-space-size=8192`. Existing manual `NODE_OPTIONS` edits to systemd service files will be overwritten on `daemon install --force` — operators relying on custom service env should use `OPENCLAW_WRAPPER`.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: 94 service-env tests pass; default heap flag generated; ambient `--require`/`--inspect`/`--expose-gc` dropped
- Edge cases checked: empty env, hostile NODE_OPTIONS, node service env path
- What you did NOT verify: Live `daemon install` on a real systemd host

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — additive change for new installs. Existing services keep their current configuration until `daemon install --force` is run.
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — this is the narrow emergency mitigation. Hard-coding 8192 MB directly addresses the OOM crash-loop reported in #96203 without adding a new config surface.
- **Refactor needed**: No — a durable heap-size surface (config key or CLI flag) is deferred to a follow-up PR.
- **Alternative considered**: `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` env var override (removed per ClawSweeper review — not durable across forced reinstall/update).

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest-risk area**: The 8192 MB default may be too high for low-memory hosts (e.g. 4 GB VPS). Mitigation: operators can use the existing `OPENCLAW_WRAPPER` escape hatch or edit the generated service file. A configurable heap surface is deferred.
- **Compatibility**: `daemon install --force` overwrites any manual `NODE_OPTIONS` edits to the generated service file. This is documented behavior — the service env is regenerated from scratch on each install.

---

Related to #96203

target.title: gateway: support user-role chat.inject with idempotent replays
target.body:
## Summary
- extend `chat.inject` params with optional `role` (`assistant` | `user`) and `idempotencyKey`
- preserve existing assistant injection behavior as default
- add a user-role transcript append path through the shared transcript append pipeline
- make idempotent `chat.inject` replays return `{ ok: true, deduped: true }` instead of failing
- add unit coverage for injected user transcript append

## Why
Telegram relay hooks need a first-class Gateway RPC to append user-role messages into a session transcript without triggering a model run.

## Notes
- backward compatible: existing `chat.inject` calls without `role` remain assistant injections
- no scope changes required (still under existing `chat.inject` authorization)

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: `chat.inject` must support user-role transcript append and idempotent replay should not fail.
- Real environment tested: Live OpenClaw Gateway on Linux host, local operator runtime, real session transcript files under `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `openclaw gateway call chat.inject --json --timeout 30000 --params '{"sessionKey":"agent:druck:telegram:group:-1003846579956:topic:1","role":"user","message":"REAL_PROOF_ROLE_USER_2026-05-13","idempotencyKey":"real-proof-user-20260513-c"}'`
  2. Repeat same command with same `idempotencyKey`.
  3. `rg -n 'real-proof-user-20260513-c|REAL_PROOF_ROLE_USER_2026-05-13' /home/aaron/.openclaw/agents/druck/sessions/d0b805cb-c944-4079-9351-3d9b620a76d2-topic-1.jsonl && rg -c 'real-proof-user-20260513-c' /home/aaron/.openclaw/agents/druck/sessions/d0b805cb-c944-4079-9351-3d9b620a76d2-topic-1.jsonl`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
$ openclaw gateway call chat.inject --json --timeout 30000 --params '{"sessionKey":"agent:druck:telegram:group:-1003846579956:topic:1","role":"user","message":"REAL_PROOF_ROLE_USER_2026-05-13","idempotencyKey":"real-proof-user-20260513-c"}'
{
  "ok": true,
  "messageId": "c7b247bc"
}

$ openclaw gateway call chat.inject --json --timeout 30000 --params '{"sessionKey":"agent:druck:telegram:group:-1003846579956:topic:1","role":"user","message":"REAL_PROOF_ROLE_USER_2026-05-13","idempotencyKey":"real-proof-user-20260513-c"}'
{
  "ok": true,
  "deduped": true
}

$ rg -n 'real-proof-user-20260513-c|REAL_PROOF_ROLE_USER_2026-05-13' /home/aaron/.openclaw/agents/druck/sessions/d0b805cb-c944-4079-9351-3d9b620a76d2-topic-1.jsonl
827:{"type":"message","id":"c7b247bc",...,"message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"REAL_PROOF_ROLE_USER_2026-05-13"}],...,"idempotencyKey":"real-proof-user-20260513-c"}}

$ rg -c 'real-proof-user-20260513-c' /home/aaron/.openclaw/agents/druck/sessions/d0b805cb-c944-4079-9351-3d9b620a76d2-topic-1.jsonl
1
```
- Observed result after fix: First call appends a user-role transcript message; second call with same `idempotencyKey` returns deduped success; transcript persists exactly one matching entry.
- What was not tested: Full end-to-end Telegram channel mention loop was not re-run as part of this proof block.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, replaying the same idempotency key returned `failed to write transcript: unknown error` for this flow.

## Hardening: race-safe idempotency (commit 9ea70b8681)

Third-party review flagged that the original `transcriptHasIdempotencyKey` precheck happened **before** acquiring the per-transcript queue+write lock — a TOCTOU window where two concurrent identical requests could both pass the check and persist duplicates. Fixed by moving the dedupe scan **inside** the locked critical section in `appendSessionTranscriptMessage` and threading `idempotencyKey` through as a first-class parameter. Result is returned with `deduped: true` and the canonical existing `messageId`, so duplicate broadcasts are suppressed too.

### Concurrent replay test (added in this PR)
`src/gateway/server-methods/chat.inject.parentid.test.ts` — 16-way `Promise.all` of identical idempotent injects:

```text
✓  gateway-methods  src/gateway/server-methods/chat.inject.parentid.test.ts (5 tests) 837ms
   ✓ dedupes concurrent identical idempotent injects (race-safe)
```

Asserts: `messageIds.size === 1`, `dedupedCount === concurrency - 1`, and the on-disk transcript contains exactly one entry with the matching `idempotencyKey`.

### Live concurrent evidence (8-way, hot-patched runtime)
```text
$ IDEM="race-final-1778710992"
$ for i in 1..8; do (openclaw gateway call chat.inject \
    --params '{"sessionKey":"...","role":"user","message":"race final","idempotencyKey":"'$IDEM'"}' \
    </dev/null >/tmp/race-$i.out 2>&1) & done; wait

[1] {"ok":true,"deduped":true}
[2] {"ok":true,"deduped":true}
[3] {"ok":true,"deduped":true}
[4] {"ok":true,"deduped":true}
[5] {"ok":true,"deduped":true}
[6] {"ok":true,"deduped":true}
[7] {"ok":true,"deduped":true}
[8] {"ok":true,"deduped":true}

# disk-state invariant: at-most-once persistence
$ grep -c "$IDEM" $TRANSCRIPT
1
$ grep "$IDEM" $TRANSCRIPT | jq -r .id | sort -u | wc -l
1
```

The on-disk invariant — at most one persisted entry per `(transcriptPath, idempotencyKey)` — holds under true concurrency.


## Post-update verification (2026-05-15)

Additional in-depth edge-case coverage was added after initial hardening:

- no-idempotency-key path does not dedupe (two writes persist)
- deduped replay does not emit duplicate transcript-update events
- assistant-role inject dedupes correctly by idempotency key
- distinct idempotency keys remain isolated (no cross-key collision)

Updated local test results on branch head `0d8ea32769`:

```text
✓  plugins  src/plugins/registry.runtime-config.test.ts (1 test)
✓  gateway-client  src/gateway/server-methods/chat.inject.parentid.test.ts (10 tests)
✓  gateway-methods  src/gateway/server-methods/chat.inject.parentid.test.ts (10 tests)
Tests  21 passed (21)
```

target.title: codex: plumb session reasoningLevel into codex model_reasoning_summary
target.body:
## Problem

The codex app-server defaults `model_reasoning_summary` to `"none"` when it isnt explicitly configured. As a result codex still produces reasoning items internally (visible in its rollout `.jsonl`) but never emits `item/reasoning/summaryTextDelta` notifications. That leaves `CodexAppServerEventProjector.handleReasoningDelta` with nothing to project — long codex turns appear silent in any UI subscribed to the gateway until the final answer arrives.

The pi-embedded runtime already maps session reasoning preferences into `reasoning.summary` via `openai-transport-stream.ts`. This brings the codex app-server path to parity.

## Repro before this change

1. Configure an agent with `agentRuntime.id: "codex"`.
2. `sessions.patch` `{ reasoningLevel: "stream" }`.
3. Send a prompt that requires multi-step reasoning.
4. Inspect `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/agent/codex-home/sessions/.../rollout-*.jsonl`: reasoning items are present but every one has `"summary": []`. The session trajectory has zero reasoning events.

## Change

- New helper `resolveCodexReasoningSummary(level)`:
  - `"off"` → `"none"`
  - `"on"` / `"stream"` → `"auto"`
  - unset / unknown → `undefined` (preserves the user's `~/.codex/config.toml` or codex's own default; no behavior change for callers that haven't opted in)
- `buildThreadStartParams` and `buildThreadResumeParams` merge `{ model_reasoning_summary }` into the thread config patch (same plumbing as `features.code_mode`).

## Tests

8 new tests in `thread-lifecycle.test.ts` covering each mapping, the unset/unknown passthrough cases, the no-clobber merge with other config keys, and the thread/resume parity path. All 35 tests in the file pass.

## Compatibility

- No schema change. `reasoningLevel` is already accepted by the `sessions.patch` schema; this only makes the codex extension honor it.
- Default behavior unchanged for sessions where `reasoningLevel` is unset — codex's own default (and any user `~/.codex/config.toml`) still wins.
- No protocol or wire-format changes; `model_reasoning_summary` is a config key codex already understands


## PR-path real behavior proof — controlled before/after on the same prompt

Captured live on an LXC running `openclaw 2026.5.7` / `@openclaw/codex 2026.5.7` / `@openai/codex 0.128.0`, model `gpt-5.5` via ChatGPT subscription OAuth. Token / email / hostname / full UUIDs redacted.

### Setup invariants (both runs)

```sh
$ cat ~/.openclaw/agents/coder/agent/codex-home/config.toml
[projects."/home/devuser/.openclaw/workspace-coder"]
trust_level = "trusted"
# (no model_reasoning_summary set — the only thing that can produce summaries
#  is the extension's thread/start config)
```

- Same agent (`agent:coder:main`, `agentRuntime.id: "codex"`)
- Same prompt: `"What is 17 times 23? Briefly explain your reasoning."`
- Same `sessions.patch` payload: `{ thinkingLevel: "medium", reasoningLevel: "stream" }`
- Binding cleared and gateway restarted between runs so each takes the `thread/start` path

---

### BEFORE — stock `@openclaw/codex 2026.5.7` bundle

Bundle confirmation:
```sh
$ grep -c 'resolveCodexReasoningSummary\|model_reasoning_summary\|reasoningSummary' \
    ~/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/dist/thread-lifecycle-CzllX4PU.js
0
```

Live gateway WS run:
```
2026-05-13T13:01:33.540Z <- connect.challenge
2026-05-13T13:01:33.542Z -> connect (sent)
2026-05-13T13:01:33.574Z <- connect ok
2026-05-13T13:01:33.574Z -> sessions.patch { reasoningLevel: stream, thinkingLevel: medium }
2026-05-13T13:01:34.229Z <- sessions.patch ok
2026-05-13T13:01:34.229Z -> chat.send
2026-05-13T13:01:34.237Z <- chat.send ok runId=before-…
```

Session pref persisted (gateway state):
```json
{ "thinkingLevel": "medium", "reasoningLevel": "stream" }
```

Codex `turn_context` (in the rollout):
```json
{ "model": "gpt-5.5", "effort": "medium", "summary": "none" }
```

Rollout event-type counts:
```
   4  reasoning            (all 4 with "summary": [])
   1  turn_context
   1  agent_message
   0  summary_text
```

**Net effect**: `sessions.patch reasoningLevel: "stream"` is accepted and persisted on the openclaw side, but the codex extension never plumbs it into the codex `thread/start` config — codex defaults `summary` to `"none"`, emits reasoning items with empty summary arrays, **zero `summary_text` events**, **zero `item/reasoning/summaryTextDelta` notifications** for `CodexAppServerEventProjector.handleReasoningDelta` to forward. Net silence to subscribers.

---

### AFTER — bundle patched with this PR's logic

Bundle patched in place to apply the diff from this PR (helper + `buildThreadStartParams` config merge); a one-line `process.stderr.write("[pr-patch] reasoningLevel=… summary=… pid=…")` probe added inside `reasoningSummaryConfigPatch` to prove the helper fires at runtime.

```sh
$ grep -c 'resolveCodexReasoningSummary\|model_reasoning_summary' \
    ~/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/dist/thread-lifecycle-CzllX4PU.js
3
$ node -c ~/.openclaw/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/dist/thread-lifecycle-CzllX4PU.js && echo syntax OK
syntax OK
```

Live gateway WS run (same payload, same prompt):
```
2026-05-13T13:03:00.445Z <- connect.challenge
2026-05-13T13:03:00.447Z -> connect (sent)
2026-05-13T13:03:00.483Z <- connect ok
2026-05-13T13:03:00.484Z -> sessions.patch { reasoningLevel: stream, thinkingLevel: medium }
2026-05-13T13:03:01.040Z <- sessions.patch ok
2026-05-13T13:03:01.041Z -> chat.send
2026-05-13T13:03:01.049Z <- chat.send ok runId=after-…
```

Gateway journal — PR helper probe fires with the session pref:
```
May 13 13:03:24 warm-… node[63576]: [pr-patch] reasoningLevel=stream summary=auto pid=63576
```

Codex `turn_context`:
```json
{ "model": "gpt-5.5", "effort": "medium", "summary": "auto" }
```

Rollout event-type counts:
```
   2  reasoning            (1 of 2 with non-empty summary)
   1  summary_text         <-- NEW
   1  turn_context
   1  agent_message
```

Captured `summary_text` content (excerpt, from the reasoning item):
```
**Checking for BOOTSTRAP**

I need to find out if BOOTSTRAP exists, especially since it seems like rg
isn't available. We didn't check with find yet, but maybe just checking if
BOOTSTRAP is there will be enough. I've noticed that the exec returned a
failure with rg. I might need to check the files using ls. This task seems
simple, but I must determine if BOOTSTRAP exists before proceeding with
any follow-up actions or deletions.
```

Final assistant answer (unchanged between runs):
```
17 × 23 = 391.

Reasoning: 17 × 20 = 340, and 17 × 3 = 51. Add them: 340 + 51 = 391.
```

---

### Side-by-side

| | session pref accepted | codex `turn_context.summary` | reasoning items / empty | `summary_text` events |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| BEFORE (stock bundle) | ✓ | `"none"` | 4 / 4 empty | **0** |
| AFTER (PR-patched bundle) | ✓ | `"auto"` | 2 / 1 empty | **1** |

The `item/reasoning/summaryTextDelta` stream this AFTER run produces is what `CodexAppServerEventProjector.handleReasoningDelta` (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts`, also visible in the installed bundle at `run-attempt-BaT_FcTv.js:1173`) already forwards as reasoning deltas to subscribed clients — no projector change needed.

### Notes

- Probe + bundle patch were applied only for proof capture on this LXC; the PR source contains the same logic at `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` (commits `6cec5562` + `8a7388dd`).
- Codex's `"auto"` mode is non-deterministic — the model decides whether a given turn warrants emitting summary text. On trivial prompts it sometimes opts out (we observed this on a couple of math turns). `"auto"` was chosen to match the existing pi-embedded behavior (`src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:1380: summary: options?.reasoningSummary || "auto"`). Forcing `"detailed"` in the patched bundle confirmed the path also produces summary text on those same trivial prompts.
- The follow-up commit (`8a7388dd`) responds to the previous review by dropping the `"off"` → `"none"` mapping, so the PR no longer clobbers a user's `~/.codex/config.toml` from openclaw's `"off"` default.


if you need more detailed logs, let me know. I can add.

target.title: fix(cli): honor ZDOTDIR/XDG_CONFIG_HOME in completion install (#63069)
target.body:
## Summary

Shell completion install/check helpers hardcoded zsh, fish, and bash profile paths under `$HOME`, ignoring `$ZDOTDIR` and `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` and disagreeing on bash `.bashrc` vs `.bash_profile`. Reporter on macOS / zsh with `$ZDOTDIR=$HOME/.config/zsh` saw completion silently written to `$HOME/.zshrc` (a file the shell never reads), so completion never activates in new shells. This PR centralizes profile-path resolution behind a single resolver that honors both env vars, treats `.bashrc`/`.bash_profile` as a priority-ordered candidate list, and lets the check and install paths agree on the same file.

## Root Cause

- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts:129` (before): `getShellProfilePath()` returned `path.join(home, ".zshrc")`, `path.join(home, ".bashrc")`, `path.join(home, ".config", "fish", "config.fish")` regardless of `$ZDOTDIR` / `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME`.
- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts:151` (before): `isCompletionInstalled()` called `getShellProfilePath(shell)` (HOME-only) and returned `false` for bash even when the install actually wrote to `.bash_profile`.
- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts:215` (before): `installCompletion()` separately hardcoded `path.join(home, ".zshrc")`, `path.join(home, ".bashrc")` / `.bash_profile` fallback, and `path.join(home, ".config", "fish", "config.fish")` — so when `.bashrc` was absent, install wrote to `.bash_profile` while check still looked at `.bashrc`, causing repeated `Enable completion?` prompts on every `openclaw update`.
- Execution path: `setup` / `update` / `completion --install` → `installCompletion()` writes to one path → `isCompletionInstalled()` (next session) checks the OTHER path → both report `false` so install is offered again, and the previously written source line lives in a file the shell never reads.
- clawsweeper review on #63069: *"Queue a focused fix PR because the report is valid, the affected files and tests are clear, and the target-repo implementation PR is closed unmerged."* and *"A shared resolver used by both status and install paths is the narrowest maintainable fix."*

## Changes

- `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts`: introduce `resolveShellProfilePathCandidates(shell, env, homeDir)` that returns candidate paths in priority order — `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc` then `$HOME/.zshrc` for zsh, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/fish/config.fish` then `$HOME/.config/fish/config.fish` for fish, `.bashrc` then `.bash_profile` for bash. Make `getShellProfilePath()` async, return the first existing candidate (or the first candidate as the canonical install target when none exist). Update `isCompletionInstalled` and `usesSlowDynamicCompletion` to `await` it. Drop the duplicate hardcoded paths in `installCompletion` and route entirely through `getShellProfilePath`, so check and install always agree on the same file.
- `src/cli/completion-runtime.profile-path.test.ts` (new): 12 colocated regression tests covering `$ZDOTDIR` zsh priority, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` fish priority, bash `.bashrc → .bash_profile` order, whitespace-only `$ZDOTDIR` handling, `os.homedir()` fallback when `env.HOME` is unset, and live `isCompletionInstalled()` against real temp directories where the OpenClaw marker lives in the non-default file.

Net diff: **+237 / −36 LOC across 2 files** (1 new test file + 1 production file).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: macOS / zsh users with `$ZDOTDIR=$HOME/.config/zsh` (a common dotfile layout, often via `/etc/zshenv` or `.zshenv`) see `openclaw completion --install` write the source line to `$HOME/.zshrc` instead of `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc`, so completion never loads in their shell. Same class of bug applies to fish users with `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` set, and to bash users who only have `~/.bash_profile` (macOS default): `isCompletionInstalled()` checks `.bashrc` while `installCompletion()` falls back to `.bash_profile`, so every `openclaw update` re-asks `Enable completion?`. Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/63069
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-65656` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, slices the patched `resolveShellProfilePathCandidates` + `getShellProfilePath` block (offset-pinned), prints its SHA-256, then runs both functions against (a) synthetic env tables matching the reporter scenarios and (b) real temp directories on disk where the OpenClaw marker is dropped into the non-default file. No mocks. PR head: `5c935bf8` on `MoerAI:fix/shell-completion-xdg-zdotdir`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/shell-completion-xdg-zdotdir && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr63069.mts`. The smoke uses `fs.mkdtemp` to build a real `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc` and `$HOME/.zshrc` pair, drops the `# OpenClaw Completion` marker into the `$ZDOTDIR` one only, and asks `getShellProfilePath("zsh", {HOME, ZDOTDIR})` which file it picks. Same for the macOS bash case where only `.bash_profile` exists.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `5c935bf8` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, patched resolver block 1961 bytes, SHA-256 `6d240bd88d09e8184ee26f26f4b7336d5a42940ecba6ce63e00e7fd65a31d3e6`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr63069.mts
=== Patched profile-path resolver — bytes from src/cli/completion-runtime.ts ===
offset: 4838 -> 6799 ( 1961 bytes)
SHA-256: 6d240bd88d09e8184ee26f26f4b7336d5a42940ecba6ce63e00e7fd65a31d3e6

=== Scenario A: ZDOTDIR set, $ZDOTDIR/.zshrc preferred ===
candidates: [ '\\home\\example\\.config\\zsh\\.zshrc', '\\home\\example\\.zshrc' ]

=== Scenario B: ZDOTDIR unset, falls back to $HOME/.zshrc ===
candidates: [ '\\home\\example\\.zshrc' ]

=== Scenario C: bash .bashrc + .bash_profile (macOS fallback) ===
candidates: [ '\\home\\example\\.bashrc', '\\home\\example\\.bash_profile' ]

=== Scenario D: XDG_CONFIG_HOME set, fish preferred ===
candidates: [
  '\\home\\example\\.alt-config\\fish\\config.fish',
  '\\home\\example\\.config\\fish\\config.fish'
]

=== Scenario E: getShellProfilePath picks the first EXISTING candidate (ZDOTDIR wins) ===
zdotdir zshrc exists: true
home zshrc exists: true
getShellProfilePath returned: C:\Users\pss\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-pr63069-VIFFw8\.config\zsh\.zshrc
matches zdotdir candidate: true

=== Scenario F: macOS bash with only .bash_profile (no .bashrc) ===
.bashrc exists: false
.bash_profile exists: true
getShellProfilePath returned: C:\Users\pss\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-pr63069-bash-Aryipc\.bash_profile
matches .bash_profile: true

=== Pass/fail summary ===
  PASS  ZDOTDIR pref
  PASS  ZDOTDIR fallback
  PASS  no ZDOTDIR -> .zshrc
  PASS  bash .bashrc first
  PASS  bash .bash_profile 2nd
  PASS  fish XDG pref
  PASS  fish fallback
  PASS  picks existing zdotdir zshrc
  PASS  macOS picks .bash_profile w/o .bashrc
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The resolver returns `$ZDOTDIR/.zshrc` first when `ZDOTDIR` is set (Scenario A), `$HOME/.zshrc` when it is not (Scenario B), `.bashrc` then `.bash_profile` for bash (Scenario C), and `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/fish/config.fish` first when set (Scenario D). The live filesystem tests confirm `getShellProfilePath("zsh", {HOME, ZDOTDIR})` actually picks the existing `$ZDOTDIR` file when both candidates exist on disk (Scenario E, this directly satisfies the reporter's macOS / zsh / `$ZDOTDIR=$HOME/.config/zsh` setup), and falls through to `.bash_profile` on a macOS-style bash setup where only that file exists (Scenario F, this satisfies the bash `.bashrc`/`.bash_profile` check/install agreement bug). All 9 assertions PASS against the patched resolver pinned by SHA-256 `6d240bd88d09e8184ee26f26f4b7336d5a42940ecba6ce63e00e7fd65a31d3e6`.
- What was not tested: An interactive `openclaw onboard` / `openclaw update` flow was not exercised on the contributor machine. The fix is a pure path-resolution change inside the existing check/install helpers; the resolver returns plain strings that flow into `fs.readFile` / `fs.writeFile` unchanged, so the only behavioral change is the file path picked. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live macOS interactive smoke is required; the reporter's issue already contains a concrete `$ZDOTDIR=~/.config/zsh` reproducer.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/cli/completion-runtime.profile-path.test.ts` — 12 new colocated regression cases (ZDOTDIR pref/fallback, whitespace-only ZDOTDIR, XDG fish pref/fallback, bash candidate order, os.homedir() fallback, plus 4 live-tempdir `isCompletionInstalled` cases covering ZDOTDIR priority, `.bash_profile`-only macOS, `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` fish, and the no-profile false case).
- `pnpm test src/cli/completion-cli.test.ts src/cli/completion-cli.write-state.test.ts src/wizard/setup.completion.test.ts` — unchanged, exercise the broader completion CLI; the resolver refactor preserves the original behavior when neither `ZDOTDIR` nor `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set and `.bashrc` exists, so existing tests continue to pass.

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix for #63069. `installCompletion()` no longer duplicates the per-shell path logic; both the check path (`isCompletionInstalled`, `usesSlowDynamicCompletion`) and the install path now share `getShellProfilePath()`, so `.bashrc` vs `.bash_profile` and `$ZDOTDIR`/`$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` stay consistent.
- Compatibility: when neither env var is set and `.bashrc` exists, the resolver returns exactly the same paths as the previous implementation, so existing installs are unaffected. Powershell paths are unchanged.
- Out of scope: `src/commands/doctor-completion.ts:111` and `:161` print hardcoded reload-hint strings (`source ~/.zshrc` etc.). Those are info-only messages on the success path and not on the failing `isCompletionInstalled` / `installCompletion` arithmetic; a separate cosmetic follow-up can route them through the same resolver.
- This follows the conservative direction in clawsweeper's review on #63069: *"A shared resolver used by both status and install paths is the narrowest maintainable fix; the closed target PR and cross-repo fork PR are useful source context, but the target repository still needs the change on main."*

Closes #63069

target.title: docs: clarify local-prefix managed Node runtime
target.body:
## Summary
- Clarify that `install-cli.sh` uses a managed local-prefix Node runtime that currently defaults to 22.22.0.
- Call out that this can intentionally differ from shell/global Node 24.
- Update install output wording so users see the managed-runtime distinction during install or update.

Closes #79558.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `bash -n scripts/install-cli.sh`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The local-prefix installer now tells users that it is using a managed Node runtime, and that this runtime may differ from their shell/global Node.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux arm64 with Node v22.21.1, running the patched `scripts/install-cli.sh` from this PR branch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
proof_tmp=$(mktemp -d)
mkdir -p "$proof_tmp/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin"
ln -s "$(command -v node)" "$proof_tmp/tools/node-v22.22.0/bin/node"
OPENCLAW_PREFIX="$proof_tmp" OPENCLAW_INSTALL_CLI_SH_NO_RUN=1 bash -c 'source scripts/install-cli.sh; install_node'
rm -rf "$proof_tmp"
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Console output from the patched installer helper:

```text
Using existing managed Node at /tmp/tmp.znUwhmDIh5/tools/node-v22.22.0; this local-prefix runtime may differ from your shell/global node.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The real installer path now surfaces the managed local-prefix Node distinction when reusing an existing managed Node, instead of silently skipping the Node step.
- **What was not tested:** I did not perform a full destructive/replacing install into a real `~/.openclaw` prefix; the proof uses a temporary local prefix to exercise the changed installer branch safely.

target.title: fix(amazon-bedrock-mantle): gate implicit discovery on AWS creds presence (#67288)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #67288. The `amazon-bedrock-mantle` plugin ran implicit Mantle provider discovery on every catalog refresh even when the user had no AWS credentials configured, emitting `[bedrock-mantle-discovery] Mantle IAM token generation unavailable` log lines on every request and attempting IAM bearer-token generation through `@aws/bedrock-token-generator` that was guaranteed to fail. The user-visible symptom is constant log noise on Raspberry Pi installs with no AWS env vars set.

The sibling `amazon-bedrock` plugin already has the gate: at `extensions/amazon-bedrock/discovery.ts:596`, implicit discovery short-circuits when `discovery.enabled !== true && !hasAwsCreds`. The mantle plugin had the `enabled === false` check but never the no-creds skip path.

## Fix

Mirror amazon-bedrock's gate in `resolveImplicitMantleProvider`:

```ts
const enabled = params.pluginConfig?.discovery?.enabled;
if (enabled === false) return null;
if (enabled !== true && !explicitBearerToken && !hasAwsSdkCreds(env)) return null;
```

Adds a small `hasAwsSdkCreds` helper that delegates to `resolveAwsSdkEnvVarName` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth-runtime` (same helper bedrock uses via `resolveBedrockConfigApiKey`). Also exports `MantleDiscoveryConfig` so the type is consumable from the registration site.

## Boundary

- Extension-local fix (`extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/`); no core or schema changes.
- No new config keys. Users who explicitly set `plugins.entries.amazon-bedrock-mantle.config.discovery.enabled: true` still get implicit discovery (and the IAM token generation attempt) even without AWS creds — opt-in for cases where the bearer token is sourced through some out-of-band mechanism.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Users running OpenClaw on hosts with no AWS credentials (Raspberry Pi, default Docker installs) reported continuous `[bedrock-mantle-discovery] Mantle IAM token generation unavailable` log lines on every request. The mantle plugin's `resolveImplicitMantleProvider` would attempt `generateBearerTokenFromIam(...)` even when neither `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` nor SDK creds were available, with the failed lookup logged on every catalog refresh. The amazon-bedrock plugin has a discovery.enabled gate plus a no-creds skip path; mantle was missing the no-creds skip.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `995ed29231` on Node 22 / Darwin 25.2.0, exercising the production `resolveImplicitMantleProvider(...)` (`extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/discovery.ts`) against a real `process.env` shape (empty, with no AWS creds) and against the SDK-creds-aware `resolveAwsSdkEnvVarName` helper from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth-runtime`. No mocks of the resolver under test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/discovery.test.ts` from the repository root, compiling the production source via tsgo and driving the live `resolveImplicitMantleProvider` against (a) empty env (no AWS creds) and (b) explicit `pluginConfig.discovery.enabled: false` with AWS bearer token set.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the `pnpm` invocation above on this exact branch:

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/shubh-trips/Documents/personal-project/open-source/openclaw

   ✓ |extension-providers| skips implicit discovery entirely when no AWS creds are present (#67288)
   ✓ |extension-providers| honors pluginConfig.discovery.enabled=false even when AWS creds are present (#67288)

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  29 passed (29)
     Duration  1.14s

  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.58s
  ```

  Runtime assertions captured by the live resolver runs: (a) with empty env (no `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK`, no SDK creds env), the resolver returns `null` AND the `tokenProviderFactory` spy is NOT called — proving we short-circuit before IAM token generation; (b) with `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` and `AWS_REGION` set but `pluginConfig.discovery.enabled: false`, the resolver still returns `null` and the token factory is not invoked — proving the explicit-disable path wins over creds presence; (c) the 27 existing discovery tests all continue to pass, including the previously-passing "returns null when no auth is available" case (the old behavior path that the user complained about — now it returns null *without* the noisy log).
- **Observed result after fix:** Installs without AWS credentials no longer see the per-request "Mantle IAM token generation unavailable" log lines and no longer attempt IAM bearer-token generation. The behavior for installs with `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` set or with full SDK creds is unchanged. The behavior for explicit `discovery.enabled: false` is unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end behavior on an actual ARM64 Raspberry Pi (the bug's reported environment) — the regression test exercises the contract layer that the per-request catalog refresh consumes, which is what produced the user-visible log spam. Downstream catalog/registration flow is unchanged.

## Risk

- **Surface:** one bundled extension (`amazon-bedrock-mantle`), discovery-only gate.
- **Backwards compat:** installs that depend on implicit Mantle discovery without AWS creds env vars need to opt in via `plugins.entries.amazon-bedrock-mantle.config.discovery.enabled: true`. The original bug report explicitly disabled mantle to work around the noise, so the documented expected migration is: either set the bearer token / SDK creds, OR set `discovery.enabled: true` to opt in to the legacy noisy behavior.

## Closes

Fixes #67288

target.title: Redact exec tool result payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- redact exec foreground tool-result text and structured `details.aggregated` before returning results to the agent
- redact node-host exec stdout/stderr/error output before formatting tool results
- redact process poll/log/list output and structured details that can include output tails or aggregated process output

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Exec and background process tool results could return secret-shaped stdout/stderr/session text back to the agent. This patch redacts those returned tool-result surfaces before they leave the exec/process/node-host formatters.

**Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/exec-tool-result-redaction` at `da701a0bbf6c`, using an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The proof used an obvious fake canary value, not a real credential.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local runtime proof that exercised `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` with a fake `REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET` value, then inspected the returned tool-result `content[0].text` and structured `details.aggregated` values for foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list tail/details surfaces.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the local runtime proof:

```json
{
  "foregroundExec": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processPoll": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n\n\nProcess exited with code 0.",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processLog": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processList": {
    "status": "completed",
    "textTail": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  }
}
```

Supplemental focused regression coverage also exercises node-host exec stdout redaction by passing a fake node `system.run` payload through `executeNodeHostCommand` and asserting both returned text and `details.aggregated` redact the raw canary.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw fake canary value was absent from returned foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list output. Redacted markers were present in the returned text/details where output was expected. The node-host formatter path is covered by the focused regression test for the same returned tool-result boundary.

**What was not tested:** Live node-host exec against a physical connected node was unavailable in this isolated proof environment; the node-host formatter was validated with the focused regression test that feeds a fake gateway node response through the production formatter path.

## Tests
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped output'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node stdout'`

Notes: a full local run of the three targeted files is currently blocked in my environment by an unrelated invalid local `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` model alias (`openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex`) during the pre-existing foreground timeout test. The new redaction regression tests pass individually under the agents-core Vitest config.

Fixes #71211



## Follow-up after review
- Added commit `bc79136bb7` to redact background exec `details.tail` before returning running-session tool results.
- Added regression coverage for background exec `details.tail`, process list command/tail details, and node-host stderr/error redaction in addition to stdout.

Additional local verification after follow-up:
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`
- `git diff --check`

Note: the full local three-file Vitest run still hits the pre-existing environment-sensitive approval path in `exec foreground failures > returns a failed text result when the default timeout is exceeded`, where local host exec approval intercepts `sleep 5` before the timeout assertion.

## Review feedback update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by removing the JSON embedded-fallback behavior change from this PR; `openclaw agent --json` fallback behavior is back to the existing contract.
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2 by replacing the live-update process timing test with deterministic `runExecProcess` regression coverage, so the previous single-line skip branches are gone.
- Final head verified: `8793847f206cca93c49abe33e4f49b9b55a0c4dc`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts -t 'JSON'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`\n\nChanged-file gate note: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` did not reach pnpm because local `crabbox` failed its binary sanity check. Direct `pnpm check:changed` also could not run in this local checkout because `origin/main...HEAD` has no merge base on the grafted/shallow branch. The focused tests, lint, and diff checks above passed on the final head.

## Process control redaction update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by redacting command-derived process result names for running-session controls (`write`, `submit`, `paste`) plus `kill`, `remove`, `poll`, `log`, `list`, and `send-keys`.
- Added fake-secret regression coverage for `process write` and `process send-keys` command-derived `details.name` values.
- Final head verified locally: `24ac21f4280e9e403722c271d12fda0a4c68535d4`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main passes a stable `directIdempotencyKey` into `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` and calls `runSubagentAnnounceDispatch` directly, while the PR proof shows repeated same-key calls produce two gateway dispatches before the wrapper and one after it.

target.title: fix(agents): dedup subagent completion announces to prevent double Slack post
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds a process-local dedup/coalesce wrapper around `deliverSubagentAnnouncement`, keyed on the announce's stable `directIdempotencyKey`, so racing lifecycle/retry paths cannot post a subagent completion twice in the requester's Slack thread.
- Failed dispatches stay retryable (only `delivered: true` results are cached); concurrent callers for the same announce share a single in-flight dispatch.
- Adds an observability log line when the cache suppresses a duplicate dispatch.

## Root cause
Multiple lifecycle paths can invoke `runSubagentAnnounceFlow` → `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` for the same `(childSessionKey, childRunId)`: initial completion, deferred descendant-settle resume, pending-final retry, resume-on-restart. The existing safeguards (gateway `agent:${idempotencyKey}` cache with 5-minute TTL, registry `completionAnnouncedAt` guard, `beginSubagentCleanup` one-shot) can be bypassed by races or by the gateway dedup TTL rolling over. With no process-local dedup at the announce boundary itself, the same announce can be dispatched twice — and each dispatch produces a user-facing post in the requester's channel.

## Fix
New `src/agents/subagent-announce-dedupe.ts` exports `runDedupedAnnounceDelivery(key, run)`:
- Namespaced keys (`deliverSubagentAnnouncement:<directIdempotencyKey>`) prevent any future cross-path collision with media-generate or orphan-recovery announces that use their own key shapes.
- 5-minute TTL matches the gateway dedup window.
- Only `delivered: true` results are cached; thrown errors and `delivered: false` outcomes release the in-flight slot so retries run fresh.
- Cache-hit and in-flight-coalesce events emit a `[subagent-announce] dedup ...` log line for ops visibility.

Wired into `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` (single change site) so all callers — subagent lifecycle, orphan recovery, media-generate background tools — get coverage.

## Test coverage
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-dedupe.test.ts` — 7 unit tests: concurrent coalesce, repeat caching, failure not cached, distinct keys isolated, undefined key passthrough, thrown error releases in-flight slot, TTL=0 disables caching.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — 4 new integration tests through the real `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` boundary:
  - Posts a Slack subagent completion only once when the same announce dispatches twice.
  - Retries delivery when the previous attempt did not produce a visible reply.
  - Retries delivery after a thrown gateway error.
  - Coalesces concurrent dispatches for the same announce key.
- `afterEach` in the delivery test now resets the new dedup cache to keep tests isolated.

## Does not affect
- Parent agent reporting paths (they do not go through `deliverSubagentAnnouncement`).
- Error reporting (`delivered: false` is not cached; errors propagate and retry).
- Progress updates (orphan-recovery uses distinct `<runId>:recovery-progress` keys per attempt, intentionally non-colliding).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: subagent task completion message is posted twice in the same Slack thread when `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` is invoked twice for the same `(childSessionKey, childRunId)` (initial complete + retry/resume path running close together, or the gateway dedup TTL rolling over between attempts).

**Real environment tested**: local OpenClaw runtime on macOS (Darwin 25.4.0, Node 24.15.0), production source loaded under `node --import tsx` — same module graph the gateway uses at runtime. Only the *outbound gateway transport* is stubbed so the test can count dispatch attempts; the dedup wrapper, dispatch router, and origin resolution all run from production source.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node --import tsx /tmp/announce-dedupe-proof.mjs        # after fix: dispatches twice via deliverSubagentAnnouncement
node --import tsx /tmp/announce-dedupe-proof-before.mjs # baseline: dispatches twice via the raw runSubagentAnnounceDispatch the patch wraps
```
Both repro scripts construct a realistic Slack-channel `directIdempotencyKey` (`announce:v1:agent:worker:subagent:child:run-live-dedup`) and a thread-bound delivery context (`channel: slack`, `to: channel:C123`, `threadId: 171.222`) and call the production module twice with identical params.

**Evidence after fix**: live console capture from `node --import tsx /tmp/announce-dedupe-proof.mjs`, showing the new dedup log line firing on the second dispatch and the stubbed gateway dispatch counter stuck at 1:
```
[repro] dispatching announce #1
[repro] #1 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro] gateway dispatches after #1: 1
[repro] dispatching announce #2 (same key)
[subagent-announce] dedup cache hit for announce:v1:agent:worker:subagent:child:run-live-dedup; suppressed duplicate dispatch (path=direct)
[repro] #2 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro] gateway dispatches after #2: 1
[repro] second result is same object as first: true
```

Before-fix comparison — same scenario but bypassing the new wrapper by driving `runSubagentAnnounceDispatch` directly (this is what `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` did before the patch):
```
[repro-before] dispatching announce #1 (no dedup)
[repro-before] #1 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro-before] gateway dispatches after #1: 1
[repro-before] dispatching announce #2 (same key, no dedup)
[repro-before] #2 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro-before] gateway dispatches after #2: 2
[repro-before] second result is same object as first: false
```

**Observed result after fix**: with the dedup wrapper in place, a second invocation of `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` for the same announce key returns the cached `delivered: true` result without re-dispatching to the gateway. Gateway dispatch counter stays at 1 across both calls; the second caller receives the same result object as the first (identity equal); the new `[subagent-announce] dedup cache hit ...` log line is emitted with the announce key and the original delivery path. The unpatched comparison shows gateway dispatches = 2 and two distinct result objects — i.e., what produced the duplicate Slack post in production.

**What was not tested**: live Slack workspace round-trip with a real bot token (I do not have a Slack workspace + OpenClaw gateway pair available here). The fix lives entirely above the channel layer — once `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` is suppressed, no Slack `chat.postMessage` is issued — so the live Slack post count is bounded by the number of times the gateway is dispatched, which the captures above show is now exactly 1.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — 44/44 passing.
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-dispatch.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-queue.test.ts src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts` — 70/70 passing.
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts` — 53/53 passing.
- `pnpm check:changed` — green (typecheck core + tests, lint core, runtime cycle and sidecar guards).
- Independent code review via the `code-reviewer` agent: safe-to-ship; namespace key + thrown-error retry test addressed.

## Test plan
- [ ] Spawn a subagent task in a Slack thread; confirm exactly one completion message lands and `[subagent-announce] dedup cache hit ...` appears in logs only on duplicated lifecycle paths.
- [ ] Force a transient announce delivery error (e.g., disconnect the gateway briefly mid-announce); confirm the eventual retry still produces exactly one final completion post and no dedup log fires for the failed first attempt.
- [ ] Cron-driven completion announces (different code path through the same `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` boundary) continue to deliver once.
